title: The Lagrangian Subspace Noise (LSN) Problem: papers, proofs, experiments, and implementations
description: Research archive for the Lagrangian Subspace Noise (LSN) problem, a variant of Learning Parity with Noise whose secret is a Lagrangian subspace of a symplectic vector space over F2. Contents: (1) the core mathematical paper 'Lagrangian Subspace Noise: Exact Statistical-Query Lower Bounds and the Linear-Reduction Landscape' (paper/lsn-core.pdf) -- exact pairwise-correlation formula, unconditional and conditional Statistical Query lower bounds, exact closed-form moment theorems for the isotropic row ensemble (with full proofs), and a near-complete impossibility landscape for linear reductions from symplectic LPN to standard LPN; (2) a companion constructions paper 'Lagrangian Subspace Noise: Cryptographic Constructions' (paper/lsn-paper.pdf) developing a succinct argument (LSN-SNARK) via the Siegel-chart representation (a key-encapsulation mechanism is left to future work), building on and citing the core paper for the hardness analysis; (3) reproducible experiments with exact-fraction data (experiments/) and Rust implementations with known-answer tests (impl/). Every claim is classified as a proved theorem, explicitly labeled empirical evidence, or an explicitly labeled conjecture. Papers and documentation are licensed CC BY 4.0; code is licensed Apache-2.0. Large-language-model assistants were used in the preparation of this work, as disclosed in the papers; the author takes full responsibility for the content.
